COMPREHENSIVE_DESC: Therapeutic ultrasound involves using sound waves to treat musculoskeletal conditions, promoting tissue healing and pain relief. It is indicated for soft tissue injuries and chronic pain conditions. Risks are minimal but may include tissue overheating.
COMORBIDITY_INFO: Complications are rare but may include tissue damage from excessive heat. Monitoring should focus on treatment duration and intensity to prevent adverse effects.

COMPREHENSIVE_DESC: Implantation of a chemotherapeutic agent involves the surgical placement of a drug delivery system, such as a wafer or pump, to deliver chemotherapy directly to a tumor site. This method is often used for brain tumors or localized cancers. Primary indications include glioblastoma and metastatic cancer. Key risks include infection, local tissue damage, and systemic toxicity.
COMORBIDITY_INFO: Common complications include local inflammation, infection, and drug resistance. Pathophysiological mechanisms involve tissue response to foreign material and drug effects. Monitoring priorities include signs of infection, drug levels, and tumor response, with clinical rationale focusing on maximizing therapeutic efficacy while minimizing adverse effects.

COMPREHENSIVE_DESC: Infusion of drotrecogin alfa (activated) is a therapeutic procedure used in the treatment of severe sepsis with organ dysfunction. It involves the administration of recombinant human activated protein C to reduce inflammation and coagulation. Primary indications include severe sepsis and septic shock. Key risks include bleeding and allergic reactions.
COMORBIDITY_INFO: Common complications include bleeding, thrombocytopenia, and allergic reactions. The pathophysiological mechanisms involve the anticoagulant and anti-inflammatory effects of the drug. Monitoring priorities include coagulation parameters and signs of bleeding to ensure safe administration.
